blockers_encountered.description: The briefing named dev/plans/prompts/0.5.4-EVENT.md as the authoritative spec and told me to verify the design note rev 4 + this prompt are present in my worktree. Neither was present: my worktree was cut from main HEAD (57010d1), but the EVENT prompt, design note rev 4 (codex PASS), Phase-A UN-28, and source context all live on feat/0.5.4-eventbus (8 commits ahead). The git-status snapshot showed design-request-event-bus.md only as an untracked file in the MAIN checkout, so it never entered the worktree.
blockers_encountered.resolution: RESOLVED — recovered the EVENT prompt (git show a474d89:...) and design note (git show 87bf11f:...) from committed history without touching the main checkout. My HEAD (57010d1) is a clean ancestor of feat/0.5.4-eventbus with zero of my own commits, so per the spec's own 'worktree fast-forward trap' instruction I ran `git merge feat/0.5.4-eventbus --ff-only`, bringing the design note rev 4 (STATUS: CODEX-GATED — PASS), the EVENT prompt, UN-28, and all READ-ONLY source files into the worktree. Verified files present, then proceeded. baseline_sha recorded as the feat train tip a220e5d that my EVENT commits stack on.

additional_changes_made_in_scope: Fast-forwarded the worktree branch from main HEAD 57010d1 to feat/0.5.4-eventbus tip a220e5d (clean fast-forward, no own commits replayed) so the EVENT pack stacks on the DESIGN/Phase-A train as intended; see blocker B1.

Anchor check: enterprise_logger.py _build_record is at lines 406-539 (spec said ~406-539) — no drift. _normalize_failure :75, _get_airlock_client :64, imports :27-35 all match. fathom mcp_arguments chain at :160-170 matches.

Added 8 extra assertion-cases beyond the 8 required scenarios (e.g. duration None when times missing, success-path bare_exception None, mcp_meta empty for normal call, frozen-event immutability) for coverage.

Post-review (codex CONCERN, 1 low finding): hardened RequestRecorder for reentrant/concurrent register() — added a threading.Lock; register()/sinks/sink_names operate under the lock; dispatch() snapshots the sink set under the lock then iterates the snapshot OUTSIDE the lock (so a slow/raising sink never holds it). Added test_reentrant_register_during_dispatch_is_snapshot_stable. 20 tests total, all in the new test file only.
agent_verify_result: pass — uv run --extra test python -m pytest tests/test_request_event.py: 20 passed (post lock-hardening). Related telemetry suite (logger/transparency/metrics + new): 148 passed, 11 skipped, 0 failed at first close. ruff check + ruff format --check clean on both changed source/test files.
next_step_for_orchestrator: Pack 0.5.4-EVENT on branch worktree-agent-a09ec693de1f81c4c at e783e0c (stacked on feat/0.5.4-eventbus a220e5d); codex CONCERN low finding addressed (recorder sink list now snapshotted under a lock). Re-review/verify, merge into feat/0.5.4-eventbus. EVENT delivers the module + tests only — no sink migrated, no registration/config touched, not installed into the live litellm callback manager (that is MIGRATE-enterprise). Then advance to the MIGRATE-* packs.
